note: Snapshot of public agentic-coding leaderboard data. Each result is the harness vendor's self-reported best published score for the named base model on the named benchmark. We aggregate; we do not re-run. See sourceUrl on each entry for the upstream report. Refreshed weekly.

benchmarks.name: SWE-bench Verified
benchmarks.description: 500 human-validated GitHub issues across 12 Python repos. The harness must produce a patch that resolves the issue and passes the project's test suite.

benchmarks.name: Terminal-Bench
benchmarks.description: Stanford and Anthropic benchmark of agentic terminal tasks. Each task gives the agent a goal and a sandboxed shell; success is measured by a deterministic post-condition.

benchmarks.name: Aider Polyglot
benchmarks.description: 225 of the hardest Exercism coding exercises across C++, Go, Java, JavaScript, Python, and Rust. Measures whole-file edit-by-diff quality, not just code generation.

benchmarks.name: SWE-Lancer
benchmarks.description: OpenAI's benchmark of paid Upwork engineering tasks ($1M+ in real bounties). Includes both diff-style fixes and longer feature work judged against the original buyer's acceptance criteria.

harnesses.name: Claude Code
harnesses.summary: Anthropic's official terminal agent. Native MCP, hooks, slash commands, subagent orchestration, and CLAUDE.md project memory. The harness most aligned with Claude's agentic post-training.

harnesses.name: Cursor Agent
harnesses.summary: VS Code fork with a multi-file agent ('Composer') and a background agent. Largest paid install base of any AI IDE; routes to whichever frontier model the user picks.

harnesses.name: Codex CLI
harnesses.summary: OpenAI's open-source terminal agent. Wraps GPT-5.5 and the o-series with code-execution sandboxing, tool-use loops, and an Apps SDK plugin model.

harnesses.name: Aider
harnesses.summary: Pioneer of edit-by-diff coding agents. Maintains its own polyglot leaderboard, runs locally, and works against any Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, or open-source model with an OpenAI-compatible API.

harnesses.name: OpenHands
harnesses.summary: Formerly OpenDevin. Open-source autonomous software engineer with a sandboxed runtime, browser tool, and microservice-style agent architecture. The reference implementation behind several SWE-bench leaderboard entries.

harnesses.name: Devin
harnesses.summary: Cognition's hosted autonomous SWE agent. Persistent VM workspaces, Slack and IDE integrations, plus a separate retrieval system (DeepWiki) over the indexed repo.

harnesses.name: Cline
harnesses.summary: Open-source VS Code agent. Plan-and-act loop, MCP support, ~$0 baseline (you pay your model provider directly). Most-installed open-source coding agent.

harnesses.name: Windsurf Cascade
harnesses.summary: Codeium's IDE agent. Cascade is the multi-step planning loop; backed by either frontier APIs or Codeium's own SWE-1 family.

harnesses.name: Amp
harnesses.summary: Sourcegraph's VS Code and JetBrains agent. Anchored on Claude Sonnet 4.6 with a code-graph-aware retrieval layer over the repo. Strong SWE-bench numbers for an IDE agent.

harnesses.name: Continue
harnesses.summary: Open-source IDE agent for VS Code and JetBrains. Configurable per-task model routing, custom slash commands, and a local-first model story. Primarily a code assistant with growing agentic features.

harnesses.name: Roo Code
harnesses.summary: Open-source VS Code agent forked from Cline. Multiple specialized 'modes' (Code, Architect, Ask, Debug), MCP support, and aggressive iteration on tool-use loops.
